steps.title: Extract two data points and calculate the rate
steps.content: From the table, use two rows: area of 560 ft\(^2\) drains 1,008 gallons, and area of 840 ft\(^2\) drains 1,512 gallons. Compute the rate (gallons per sq ft): $$\frac{1{,}512 - 1{,}008}{840 - 560} = \frac{504}{280} = 1.8$$

steps.title: Confirm the function passes through the origin
steps.content: Zero roof area produces zero drainage, so \(f(0) = 0\). The function has no constant term: \(f(x) = 1.8x\).

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: \(f(x) = 1.8x\) matches choice B. Verify: \(f(560) = 1.8 \times 560 = 1{,}008\). \(f(840) = 1.8 \times 840 = 1{,}512\). Both confirmed.

explanation:
• Choice A — 'f(x) = 0.6x': At x = 560, gives 336 gallons, but the table shows 1,008. ✗
  • Choice B — 'f(x) = 1.8x': At x = 560, gives 1,008; at x = 840, gives 1,512. Both match the table exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'f(x) = 504.0x': At x = 560, gives 282,240 gallons — vastly too large. ✗
  • Choice D — 'f(x) = 1,008.0x': At x = 560, gives 564,480 gallons — vastly too large. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
